title: $curocc
text: $curocc Return the sequence number of the current occurrence in the hitlist. $curocc { ( Entity ) } Example: message "Number %%$curocc of %%$totocc" Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Name of the entity. If omitted, the current entity is used. Return Values Sequence number in the hitlist of the current occurrence. Empty string (""), if an error occurred, in which case $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 2. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following $curocc Value Error constant Meaning -1102 <UPROCERR_ENTITY> The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not painted on the component. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The following events affect the value of $curocc : Table 3. Events that change $curocc Event Action Comment discard No change. If there is an occurrence following the discarded occurrence. Note: If discard is used outside the read trigger, $status may return 0 (indicating that there are no following occurrences) even when there are still occurrences to be read. For more information, see discard . Subtracts 1 from $curocc . If there is no occurrence following the discarded occurrence. retrieve Sets $curocc to 1. setocc Sets $curocc to specified occurrence. ^ADD_OCC Adds 1 to $curocc . $totocc is increased by 1. ^INS_OCC No change. $totocc is increased by 1. ^NEXT_OCC Adds 1 to $curocc . If there is a next occurrence present in the component. No change. If there is no next occurrence. ^PREV_OCC Subtracts 1 from $curocc . If there is a previous occurrence present in the component. No change. If there is no previous occurrence. Example: Using $curocc The following example shows ProcScript in the getFocus trigger of an entity. The $curocc and $totocc are used to show the user the position of the current occurrence in relation to other occurrences in the component structure: trigger getFocus message "Number %%$curocc of %%$totocc" end Copy Related Links creocc Creates an empty occurrence of the specified entity. remocc Mark an occurrence of the specified entity for deletion at the next store. $dbocc Return the sequence number of the current occurrence within the set of occurrences retrieved from the database. setocc Make a specific occurrence the current occurrence. $totocc Return the number of occurrences of an entity in the component.
